Heatwave paralyses life in Adilabad region; temperatures touch 46.5 degrees Celsius
Severe heatwave conditions continued in the erstwhile Adilabad region on Thursday, with temperatures reaching up to 46.5°C, according to TSDPS data. Normal life was disrupted, with reports of a sunstroke death, stalled farming activity, and heat-related incidents across the region.
Adilabad: Parts of erstwhile Adilabad continued to boil under scorching heat wave conditions for the second day on Thursday, almost disrupting normal life.
According to the weather report on the website of Telangana Development Planning Society, Dilawarpur in Nirmal district saw the highest maximum temperature in Telangana with 46.5 degrees Celsius on Wednesday. Both Koutala and Devulawada registered the maximum temperature of 46.3 degrees C each. Jainad recorded the maximum temperature at 46.2 degrees C. The region witnessed sweltering heat wave conditions on Thursday.
Following torrid heat wave conditions, people were forced to stay indoors. They consumed fruit juices, coconut water and butter milk to beat the heat. Farmers suspended agriculture activities. Roads in towns and mandal centres wore deserted looks.
A farm laborer Naini Pochanna (30) died of sunstroke at Devapur village in Thalamadugu mandal on Wednesday. He experienced nausea and vomiting after returning from his agriculture field. He was immediately shifted to a hospital. He breathed his last while undergoing treatment.
A two-wheeler went up in flames when fire caught the vehicle in the forests of Nelwai village in Vemanapalli mandal on Wednesday afternoon. The bike rider, Shyam managed to survive by jumping off the bike on noticing the blaze. The two-wheeler caught fire supposedly caused by a scorching heat wave. It was reduced to ashes even before firefighters could arrive at the spot.
Meanwhile, smoke emanated from the front tyres of an Afiabad-bound TGRTC bus at Kerameri mandal centre, triggering panic among passengers. However, the driver doused the smoke by pouring water on the tyres. The passengers heaved a sigh of relief. The bus originated at Adilabad town.

Heatwave tightens grip on Telangana; Khammam sizzles at 45 degree Celsius
Telangana continues to reel under intense heatwave conditions, with Khammam recording the highest temperature at 45°C, according to IMD Hyderabad data. Several northern and eastern districts, including Ramagundam, Nizamabad and Adilabad, also reported extreme daytime heat and warm night conditions.
Hyderabad: Heatwave conditions across Telangana continued with Khammam, in the last 24-hours recording the State’s highest at 45 degree Celsius, India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad weather data on Thursday said.
Meanwhile, Hakimpet recording highest in Hyderabad at 41.3 degrees Celsius.
According to IMD data, a heatwave has formed over northern and eastern districts of Telangana. After Khammam, the next district to record the highest temperature (in degree Celsius) was Ramagundam (44.6), Nizamabad (44.4), and Adilabad at 44.3.
The knorthern districts including Adilabad and Nizamabad experienced severe warm night conditions, with minimum temperatures at 32.7 and 32.3 respectively.
According to IMD weather data, Hyderabad recorded a maximum of 39.5 i.e. Begumpet station, while other weather monitoring stations at Hakimpet recorded 41.3, Dundigal recorded 41 degrees Celsius.

title: Heatwave Continues In Telangana, Red Alert Issued For Several Districts - INDToday
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Telangana
full_text:
Hyderabad, May 21: Telangana continues to reel under intense heatwave conditions as soaring temperatures turn several districts into virtual furnaces. With the impact of summer intensifying sharply, people are facing extreme discomfort from early morning hours itself.
Weather experts warned that temperatures are likely to rise further on Wednesday, with severe heatwave conditions expected across many districts. Popular weather analyst Balaji said the Sun’s intensity has increased significantly from as early as 6 am, leaving residents struggling to cope with the harsh weather.
According to forecasts, temperatures in some districts may touch a scorching 47 degrees Celsius. Authorities cautioned people against venturing outdoors between 11 am and 4 pm due to the high risk of heatstroke and dehydration.
The districts placed under Red Alert include Adilabad, Komaram Bheem Asifabad, Nirmal, Jagtial, Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Rajanna Sircilla, Nizamabad, Jayashankar Bhupalpally, Hanamkonda, Mulugu, Warangal, Jangaon, Bhadradri Kothagudem, Mahabubabad, Nalgonda, Suryapet and Khammam.
Hyderabad May Cross 43 Degrees Amid Heatwave
Meteorologists said daytime temperatures in these districts are likely to range between 44 and 47 degrees Celsius. Officials advised residents to stay indoors as much as possible, drink plenty of water and avoid direct exposure to sunlight during peak afternoon hours.
In Hyderabad, temperatures are also expected to cross 43 degrees Celsius, adding to the discomfort in the city. Warmer nights are further worsening the situation as residents are getting little relief even after sunset.
Meanwhile, some districts may witness isolated thunderstorms and light rainfall. Weather experts predicted rain accompanied by thunder in Vikarabad, Ranga Reddy, Sangareddy, Nagarkurnool, Wanaparthy, Jogulamba Gadwal and Mahabubnagar districts.
Officials urged people, especially children, elderly persons and those with health conditions, to take extra precautions until weather conditions improve.

title: Telangana weather today: IMD issues severe heatwave alert for 14 districts, Hyderabad temperature to touch - The Economic Times
full_text:
A red alert for severe heatwave is in place for Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Peddapalli, Karimnagar, Jagtial, Kamareddy, Rajanna Sircilla, Nizamabad, Nirmal, Suryapet and Nalgonda. Telangana recorded 46.5 degrees Celsius for the first time this summer season, marking its highest temperature yet.
Rainfall likely in some places
Besides the heatwave warning, the IMD also forecast light to moderate rainfall over the next three days. The IMD stated that light to moderate rainfall is likely at isolated places in some districts today and tomorrow, accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds. However, dry weather conditions are expected to prevail from the following day onwards.84 locations report heatwave in Telangana

The situation turned even more severe in two places—one each in Jangaon and Kumuram Bheem Asifabad districts—where authorities recorded severe heatwave conditions.According to data from the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, Dilawarpur in Nirmal district emerged as the hottest location, recording a maximum temperature of 46.5°C. title: Think 46°C is hot in Telangana? IMD says brace for even more over next two days - The New Indian Express
full_text:
HYDERABAD: More than half of Telangana reeled under a severe heatwave on Wednesday, with at least 20 districts recording temperatures above 46°C. The highest temperature in the state was recorded at Dilawarpur in Nirmal district at 46.5°C. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) said the heatwave conditions are likely to continue for the next four days.
In Hyderabad, the highest temperature was recorded at Saidabad at 42.4°C.
The IMD also forecast light to moderate rain accompanied by thunderstorms in parts of the state over the next two days. Scattered rainfall is expected in a few districts on Thursday, while dry weather conditions are likely to prevail on Friday. Maximum temperatures are expected to rise gradually by 1–2°C over the next two days.
Meteorologists said conditions remain favourable for the further advancement of the southwest monsoon over parts of the southeast Arabian Sea, Comorin region, Bay of Bengal and Andaman Sea during the next three to four days.
According to the weather department, a trough extending from the southeast Arabian Sea to the central Bay of Bengal via Lakshadweep, Kerala, Karnataka and Andhra Pradesh is influencing weather conditions over the region. Another trough extending from east Uttar Pradesh to north Telangana is also affecting the weather pattern.
The weather office said an upper air cyclonic circulation over south coastal Andhra Pradesh and adjoining areas has weakened, leading to changes in atmospheric conditions over Telangana.

title: Telangana heatwave alert: Mercury may hit 47°C in district, Hyd to record around 42°C - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning for Telangana on May 20.
Mercury is expected to cross the 44°C mark in several districts, while independent weather analysts warn that northern and eastern pockets could see localized peaks as high as 47°C.
According to the latest bulletin from the Meteorological Centre in Hyderabad, heatwave conditions are very likely to prevail in isolated pockets of Adilabad, Komuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial, Nirmal, Nizamabad, and Jagtial. While the official forecast for Hyderabad city places the maximum temperature between 41°C and 44°C, local experts suggest the capital is likely to hit 43°C today, urging citizens to take necessary precautions against heat exhaustion and dehydration.
Brace for thunderstorms by evening
The day's weather is expected to take a sharp turn by late evening. Despite the intense heat, the IMD has signaled a high probability of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds reaching speeds of 40-50 kmph. These isolated but intense thunderstorms are expected to impact districts including Rangareddy, Nalgonda, Nagarkurnool, Vikarabad, Sangareddy, and Wanaparthy. For Hyderabad, the forecast predicts a partly cloudy sky with light rain or dry conditions likely towards the night, offering only marginal relief from the day's blistering heat.
Stay safe, stay cool
In response to the rising temperatures, the IMD has also issued an advisory, "Stay Safe, Stay Cool". The advisory emphasized the dangers of a prolonged heatwave. Health officials recommend that the public avoid outdoor exposure between 12:00 PM and 3:00 PM, which is considered the peak period for heat intensity.
Citizens are encouraged to wear light-colored, loose cotton clothing, use umbrellas or hats, and ensure they remain hydrated by drinking plenty of water, even in the absence of thirst.
The agricultural and animal husbandry sectors have also been placed on alert. Farmers are advised to provide frequent irrigation to standing crops, while cattle owners are urged to keep animals in the shade and avoid grazing during the noon hours. With temperatures remaining significantly above normal, the IMD continues to monitor the situation closely, advising residents to stay updated through official channels and the Mausam mobile app.

title: Intense heatwave and thunderstorm alert grips Telangana; Scattered rains forecast - Dailyhunt
full_text:
Hyderabad: Amid the intense summer heat, Adilabad sizzled at 44.3 degree Celsius topping the high temperatures list of Telangana on Wednesday.
Day temperatures remained high across most parts of the state with Nizamabad recording 44.2 degree Celsius, Medak logging in 42.6 degree Celsius, Khammam with 42.4 degree Celsius and and Mahabubnagar recording 41.9 degree Celsius.
The grueling heatwave continues to dominate with maximum temperatures soaring past 44 degree celsius in northern districts and 40 degree Celsius in Hyderabad.
While the India Meteorological Department (IMD), Hyderabad, has forecast isolated thunderstorms, the mercury is expected to remain high in several regions.
Hyderabad remains under orange alert as temperatures hover above 40 degree Celsius. In the last 24 hours, Hayathnagar emerged as the hottest region at 41 degree Celsius followed by Hakimpet (40.8) Rajendra Nagar (40.5), Begumpet (40.4) and Patancheru (39.4).
The IMD, Hyderabad forecast indicated that isolated places in Hyderabad, Medchal-Malkajgiri, Rangareddy, and Sangareddy are likely to experience thunderstorms and gusty winds on Thursday evening or early hours of Friday.
Despite the potential for localized rain, the IMD warns that there will be no large change in maximum temperatures for the next three days. Looking ahead to May 3, the heatwave conditions are projected to intensify further, with temperatures in Hyderabad and surrounding districts expected to range between 41 degree Celsius and 44 degree Celsius.

title: Telangana Heatwave Claims 9 Lives As Temperatures Cross 46°C - INDToday
full_text:
Hyderabad, May 21: Telangana is reeling under severe heatwave conditions after temperatures crossed 46 degrees Celsius in several districts, with at least nine people reportedly dying due to sunstroke-related incidents.
The highest temperature was recorded at Dilawarpur in Nirmal district at 46.5°C on Wednesday. Districts including Karimnagar, Peddapalli, Hanamkonda, Jagtial, Khammam and Nizamabad also witnessed extreme temperatures above 46°C.
According to reports, six deaths linked to heatstroke were reported from the combined Karimnagar region. The victims included elderly persons, farmers and a differently abled man. Additional deaths were reported from Mahabubabad, Mulugu and Narayanpet districts.
IMD Issues Red Alert Over Telangana Heatwave
The India Meteorological Department has issued a Red Alert for Telangana as temperatures continue to remain 5 to 6 degrees above normal levels.
Officials warned that severe heatwave conditions are likely to persist for the next two days. Residents of Adilabad, Karimnagar, Warangal, Khammam, Nizamabad and Nalgonda districts have been advised to take precautions.
Doctors urged elderly people, children, pregnant women and individuals with existing health conditions to avoid stepping outdoors during afternoon hours.
The extreme heat also triggered unusual incidents in parts of the state. A motorcycle reportedly caught fire while moving through a forest area in Mancherial district. In Kumuram Bheem Asifabad district, smoke emerged from an RTC bus tyre, though the driver managed to prevent a major mishap.
Authorities advised people to stay indoors during peak daytime hours, drink sufficient water and avoid direct exposure to sunlight.

title: Telangana heatwave kills nine as temperatures soar - Hyderabad Mail
full_text:
HYDERABAD: Nine people died of suspected heatstroke across Telangana on Wednesday as severe heatwave conditions gripped north and central districts of the state. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued a red alert across Telangana and warned that the extreme temperatures would continue for the next two days.
North Telangana districts record extreme temperatures
The highest temperature of 46.5 degrees Celsius was recorded at Dilawarpur in Nirmal district. Temperatures crossed 45 degrees Celsius in 18 districts, with several areas recording heat levels 5-6 degrees above normal.
Officials said the worst-hit regions included the undivided Adilabad, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Warangal, Khammam and Nalgonda districts. Doctors advised elderly people, children, pregnant women and those with health conditions to avoid stepping outdoors during peak daytime hours.
Six deaths reported in undivided Karimnagar district
Among the victims were woman farmer Nerragaddam Lachavva, 55, of Gollapalli village in Konaraopet mandal of Rajanna Sircilla district, and Enge Raj Reddy, 60, of Arepalli in Vemulawada mandal.
Others who died due to suspected heatstroke included Kandukuri Rajamma, 83, of Kolanoor in Odela mandal of Peddapalli district; farmer Sama Lachireddy, 60, of Narsingapur in Dharmaram mandal; Kanukuntla Kanakaiah, 75, of Kothapalli in Peddapalli mandal; and differently-abled person Gajarla Satish, 45, from the 21st ward of Huzurabad.
In Mahabubabad district, Nerella Somaiah, 72, from Arpanapalli village in Kesamudram mandal died due to heatstroke. Sanitation worker Joda Sambaiah from Premnagar in Mulugu municipality and Dasari Hanmanthu, 50, from Maddelabeed village in Damaragidda mandal of Narayanpet district also died.
Red alert issued across Telangana
The IMD said heatwave conditions intensified from early morning on Wednesday, leaving people struggling across Telangana. Authorities urged residents to remain indoors during afternoon hours and stay hydrated.
The following temperatures were recorded across the state on Wednesday:
- Megullapalli, Jayashankar Bhupalpally district – 45.8°C
The IMD data also showed some of the highest temperatures recorded in the state over the decades, including 48.6 degrees Celsius in Bhadradri Kothagudem district in 1973 and 47.8 degrees Celsius in Hanamkonda on May 6, 2023.
